english: to grasp / to seize / to hold / to comprehend / to contain
notes: Has many meanings: physically grabbing something, mentally comprehending something, or a container holding a certain amount. Also used in fixed expressions like 'einen Entschluss fassen' (to make a decision) and 'Mut fassen' (to pluck up courage).

sentences.german: Die Polizistin fasste den Verdächtigen am Arm.
sentences.english: The police officer grabbed the suspect by the arm.

sentences.german: Er hat endlich einen Entschluss gefasst.
sentences.english: He has finally made a decision.

sentences.german: Ich kann es kaum fassen, dass wir gewonnen haben.
sentences.english: I can hardly comprehend that we won.
